question_text: A seesaw is balanced with a 40 N load sitting 2 metres from the pivot on the left side. A child applies an effort force on the right side, 4 metres from the pivot, to keep the seesaw balanced. Calculate the effort force needed.
explanation: Using the principle of moments for a balanced lever: load force × load distance = effort force × effort distance. Rearranging: effort force = (load force × load distance) ÷ effort distance = (40 × 2) ÷ 4 = 80 ÷ 4 = 20 N. The child needs to push with 20 N of force.
